titleEn: Poultry processing injury rates hit record low in 2024
titleZh: 2024年美国家禽加工工伤率创历史新低
summaryZh: 美国劳工统计局数据显示，2024年家禽加工行业可记录工伤与疾病率降至每100名全职员工2.4例，为1994年有记录以来最低，且低于所有主要工业、制造业及食品制造业水平。该成就归功于行业在人体工学、自动化、培训及早期干预项目上的持续投资。联合家禽行业安全与健康委员会表示将继续提升员工健康保障。
featuredReason: 该新闻展示了家禽加工行业通过自动化、人体工学等技术创新显著降低工伤率的成功案例，为智慧畜牧领域在安全管理和效率提升方面提供了可借鉴的实践路径。
contentFull: Processing Facilities and people Health and safety (processing) Poultry processing injury rates hit record low in 2024 BLS data shows sector outperforming all major industries 29 January 2026 1 minute read By: Global Ag Media North America The incidence of occupational injuries and illnesses within the US poultry sector’s slaughter and processing workforce has fallen below all general industry, manufacturing and food manufacturing levels again since the Department of Labor’s Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) began recording injuries and illnesses information in 1994, according to a recent press release from the National Chicken Council. The total recordable poultry processing illness and injury rate for 2024 is 2.4 cases per 100 full-time workers annually, the lowest rate recorded by the Bureau of Labor Statistics for poultry processing. The poultry industry’s rate of 2.4 was below the rate of 3.2 for similar agricultural industries in terms of injuries per 100 full-time workers and lower than 3.3 for the entire food manufacturing sector, all manufacturing industries at 2.7, and all general industry at 2.6.Poultry processing’s 2024 rate of 2.4 represents a 90% decrease from 1994 (the oldest data available on the BLS website), when the recorded rate was 22.7. Below are the poultry processing incident rates for 2024.“Poultry processing has achieved the lowest injury and illness rates ever recorded by BLS, confirming what our industry has worked toward for decades. This milestone reflects sustained investments in ergonomics, automation, training, and early intervention programs that put employee health first. While we are proud of this progress, our commitment to continuous improvement in workplace safety remains unwavering,” said the Joint Poultry Industry Safety & Health Council.The Joint Poultry Industry Safety & Health Council consists of members from the U.S. Poultry & Egg Association (USPOULTRY), the National Chicken Council (NCC) and the National Turkey Federation (NTF). Collectively, the three organizations represent companies that produce 95% of the nation’s poultry products and directly employ more than 350,000 workers.“Protecting our workforce is central to everything we do,” said USPOULTRY, NCC and NTF. “The latest Bureau of Labor Statistics data shows that poultry processing continues to outperform general industry, manufacturing, and food manufacturing in incident rates — an achievement that reflects years of focused investment in safety, training and innovation. While we are pleased with this progress, we remain committed to raising the bar even higher to safeguard the health and well-being of our employees.” Our Partners World Poultry Science Association Congress Poultry Science Association Targan dsm-firmenich Lallemand Lanxess BioZyme Hy-Line International Global Ag Media More from this author More News View all Australia ramps up avian influenza testing Health & disease Diseases Avian influenza 24 June 2026 1 minute read H5N1 spreads to second Australian state Health & disease Diseases Avian influenza 24 June 2026 1 minute read Cases confirmed in South Australia after dead seabirds found Canada launches $3B food security strategy to boost domestic output Markets & policy Policy and regulation Food security 24 June 2026 1 minute read Plan targets grocery competition, local production and red tape cuts Our Partners World Poultry Science Association Congress Poultry Science Association Targan dsm-firmenich Lallemand Lanxess BioZyme Hy-Line International
translationZh:
美国国家鸡肉委员会近日发布的新闻稿称，自美国劳工部劳工统计局（BLS）1994年开始记录工伤与疾病信息以来，美国家禽屠宰与加工行业的职业伤害与疾病发生率再次低于所有一般工业、制造业及食品制造业水平。

2024年家禽加工可记录工伤与疾病率为每100名全职员工每年2.4例，这是劳工统计局记录的家禽加工行业最低水平。家禽行业2.4的比率低于类似农业行业（每100名全职员工3.2例）、整个食品制造业（3.3例）、所有制造业（2.7例）以及所有一般工业（2.6例）。

家禽加工行业2024年2.4的比率相比1994年（BLS网站上可查的最早数据）的22.7下降了90%。以下是2024年家禽加工事故率数据。

**联合家禽行业安全与健康委员会**表示：“家禽加工行业实现了劳工统计局有记录以来最低的工伤与疾病率，证实了我们行业数十年来努力工作的成果。这一里程碑反映了在人体工学、自动化、培训及早期干预项目上的持续投入，这些项目将员工健康放在首位。虽然我们为这一进展感到自豪，但我们对持续改善工作场所安全的承诺坚定不移。”

联合家禽行业安全与健康委员会由美国禽蛋协会（USPOULTRY）、国家鸡肉委员会（NCC）和国家火鸡联合会（NTF）的成员组成。这三个组织共同代表生产全美95%家禽产品的公司，直接雇佣超过35万名员工。

USPOULTRY、NCC和NTF表示：“保护我们的员工是我们一切工作的核心。最新的劳工统计局数据显示，家禽加工行业在事故率方面继续优于一般工业、制造业和食品制造业——这一成就反映了多年来在安全、培训和创新方面的集中投入。虽然我们对这一进展感到满意，但我们仍致力于进一步提高标准，以保障员工的健康与福祉。”
contentHtml:
Poultry processing injury rates hit record low in 2024 
BLS data shows sector outperforming all major industries
